parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff removes a library module (`process.mjs`), adds 371 lines of new retry/error infrastructure, and modifies state management — all without the reviewer being able to verify that no dangling imports, misclassified errors, or non-atomic state writes exist. The deleted module is the most concrete ship-blocker; the retry and state changes are high-regression surface areas that need targeted verification.

findings.title: Deleted `scripts/lib/process.mjs` may leave dangling runtime imports
findings.body: The diff removes `scripts/lib/process.mjs` (67 lines) entirely. The commit message 'Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge' suggests intentional cleanup, but `scripts/check-imports.mjs` was only modified by 5 lines — which may have relaxed the import lint rather than confirmed all consumers were migrated. If any runtime path, hook script, or test still does a static or dynamic import of `./lib/process` or `./lib/process.mjs`, the module will crash at load time with MODULE_NOT_FOUND. This class of failure is silent in CI if no test exercises the affected import path.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for any remaining reference to 'process.mjs' (import, require, dynamic import, or string literal). Verify that check-imports.mjs changes didn't simply exclude the deleted file from validation. Add an explicit test that confirms no import resolves to the deleted path.

findings.title: New retry infrastructure (203 lines) may retry non-idempotent operations
findings.body: `scripts/lib/retry.mjs` adds 203 lines of retry/backoff logic. It is consumed by `glm-client.mjs` (155 lines changed). LLM API calls that create resources — e.g., submitting reviews, creating jobs — may not be idempotent. If the retry layer wraps such calls without deduplication keys or idempotency tokens, a transient timeout followed by a retry could result in duplicate submissions. The test file (`tests/retry.test.mjs`, 352 lines) likely tests the retry mechanism itself but may not cover the integration with non-idempotent glm-client call sites.
findings.recommendation: Audit every call site in glm-client.mjs where retry is applied. Classify each as idempotent (GET/status) vs non-idempotent (POST/create). For non-idempotent calls, either add idempotency keys, make the operation safe to replay, or exclude it from automatic retry and surface the error to the user instead.

findings.title: BigModel error dispatch may silently misclassify unknown error codes
findings.body: `scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs` (168 new lines) introduces error classification for BigModel API responses. If the classifier uses a whitelist of known error codes and falls through to a default that either silently retries non-retryable errors or gives up on retryable ones, the behavior is wrong but not obviously so — it would manifest as either wasted API calls or dropped work. The 189-line test file likely covers known codes but may not exercise the unknown-code fallback path.
findings.recommendation: Verify the default/fallback branch in the error classifier explicitly logs the unrecognized error code and treats it as non-retryable (fail-safe). Add a test case for an unrecognized error code to confirm it does not silently retry or swallow the error.

findings.title: State and tracked-jobs file writes may not be atomic under concurrency
findings.body: `scripts/lib/state.mjs` (32 lines changed), `scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs` (78 lines changed), and `scripts/lib/fs.mjs` (44 lines changed) all touch persistent state. If background jobs (--background flag added in v0.4.5) run concurrently with foreground operations, two processes may read-modify-write the same state file simultaneously. Without file locking or atomic rename patterns, this can corrupt the state file or silently lose job entries. The diff adds a --background mode which directly increases concurrent-access probability.
findings.recommendation: Verify that all state mutations in state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs use an atomic write pattern (write to temp file, then rename). Consider adding advisory file locking (flock/lockfile) for --background mode where concurrent access is expected. Add a test that simulates concurrent state mutations.

next_steps: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs\|process\.js\|lib/process' scripts/ tests/ commands/` to confirm no dangling imports to the deleted module.
Audit each glm-client.mjs call site wrapped by retry for idempotency; document which are safe to retry and which are not.
Add a test for unknown/unrecognized BigModel error codes to bigmodel-errors.test.mjs confirming fail-safe behavior.
Verify atomic write semantics in state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs — check for write-then-rename or equivalent patterns.
Run the full test suite (`scripts/ci/check-all.sh` or equivalent) against the cumulative diff before merge.
